items.hook: Ralph Waldo Emerson delivers the Divinity School Address at Harvard Divinity School, discounting Biblical miracles and declaring Jesus a great man, but not God. The Protestant community reacts with outrage.
items.body: Ralph Waldo Emerson, who went by his middle name Waldo, was an American essayist, lecturer, philosopher, minister, abolitionist, and poet who led the Transcendentalist movement of the mid-19th century. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Spanish Inquisition is officially disbanded after nearly 356 years.
items.body: The Tribunal of the Holy Office of the Inquisition was authorized by Pope Sixtus IV in 1478 and the first inquisitors, Miguel de Morillo and Juan de San Martín, were appointed by the future Catholic Monarchs, King Ferdinand II of Aragon and Queen Isabella I of Castile, in 1480. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Massacre of July 15, 1927: Eighty-nine protesters are killed by Austrian police in Vienna.
items.body: The July Revolt of 1927 was a major riot starting on 15 July 1927 in the Austrian capital, Vienna. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: It ensued after seven Catholic cantons formed the Sonderbund in 1845 to protect their interests against a centralization of power.
items.body: The Sonderbund War of November 1847 was a civil war in Switzerland, then still a relatively loose confederacy of cantons. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Swedish–Novgorodian Wars: A Novgorodian army led by Alexander Nevsky defeats the Swedes in the Battle of the Neva.
items.body: The Swedish–Novgorodian Wars were a series of armed conflicts in the 12th and 13th centuries between the Novgorod Republic and medieval Sweden over control of the Gulf of Finland. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Drive Your Plow Over the Bones of the Dead is a 2009 mystery novel by Olga Tokarczuk.
